drivers.label: Revenue growth · Yr 1
drivers.comment: GOOGL grew ~15% in FY25 (revenue $402.8B). Base ≈ trend; Bull assumes AI/Cloud re-accelerates. Reasonable across the range.

drivers.label: Revenue growth · Yr 10
drivers.comment: Fades to mature growth by year 10. 6% (Base) on a $700B+ revenue base is sensible; 3% (Bear) is conservative.

drivers.label: EBIT margin · Yr 10
drivers.comment: Assumes steady operating-margin expansion despite heavy AI capex. 39% (Bull) would be an all-time high — plausible only if AI drives real leverage.

drivers.label: WACC
drivers.comment: ERP 3.9–4.3% ≈ Damodaran's implied 4.23% (2026); beta ~1.0–1.1. This is where current market conditions legitimately enter the model.

drivers.label: Terminal growth
drivers.verdict: Base OK · Bull at the ceiling
drivers.comment: Terminal g should not exceed the ~4% risk-free / long-run nominal GDP. Bull's 3.5% sits right at that edge.

drivers.label: Capex · Yr 1 (% rev)
drivers.comment: Near-term capex is elevated for the AI data-centre build-out. The assumed decline over the forecast is the single biggest swing factor.

drivers.label: Net working capital (% rev)
drivers.comment: GOOGL actually runs near-zero / negative working capital (deferred revenue funds it). Modelling a 15–18% drag understates value — the model is conservative here.

drivers.label: Scenario probability
drivers.comment: Weights already lean bullish (Bull 40% / Base 45%). The market's high-20s× multiple implies even more weight on the Bull path — that's the AI fever, priced.

comps.note: The chosen multiple is where 'market hotness' legitimately belongs — not a fudge factor on the DCF. At 25× (a quality-tech multiple) comps land almost exactly on the mid-year DCF, so both methods triangulate near the same value. The market currently pays a high-20s× forward multiple; that premium over the central estimate IS the AI-regime optimism, made explicit and auditable rather than hidden in a sentiment multiplier.
